objective: Close the remaining reachable F-0163 answer-write failure by replacing unconditional personal AI learning answer upserts with one authoritative revision-and-command-digest CAS contract that returns persisted facts, rejects stale devices and preserves response-loss replay without changing authorization, scoring or F-0164 lifecycle semantics.
blockedFiles: any migration apply, real database/data, Provider/network call, permission, entitlement, quota, Prompt, dependency, deployment, secret/env, PR, force-push or mechanism change

preRedImpactScan.authoritativeChain: authorized answer request -> immutable owned learning session and exact question -> canonical server-scored feedback -> database revision and command-digest CAS -> persisted authoritative feedback response and progress
preRedImpactScan.currentVulnerableSink: personal_ai_learning_answer_feedback uses an unconditional session-question upsert; a stale or concurrent request can overwrite newer feedback and service returns the pre-persistence local object instead of the authoritative row
preRedImpactScan.alreadyCovered: session creation now rereads and returns the unique persisted winner with exact context matching; F-0162 makes first paper-session input immutable, so only the answer-write CAS and monotonicity half of F-0163 remains reachable
preRedImpactScan.securityInvariant: route authorization and object masking happen before answer facts are read or mutated; actor, session and question identity remain server-validated and no cross-owner state is disclosed
preRedImpactScan.legitimateBehavior: first answer starts at revision 1; an exact response-loss replay is read-only; a client that has reloaded the latest revision may intentionally submit a new answer; stale or conflicting revisions never mutate persisted feedback
preRedImpactScan.bundleDisposition: F-0163 answer CAS is selected independently; F-0164 lifecycle, history and statistics remains pending after this prerequisite; F-0013 remains runtime_hold

requiredSemantics.schema: add exactly two legacy-nullable answer feedback fields, answer_revision and answer_command_digest, with one strict all-null legacy or complete-current CHECK; current revision is a positive database integer and digest is lowercase sha256; no default, backfill, data SQL or destructive DDL
requiredSemantics.request: current answer requests carry an exact nonnegative expectedAnswerRevision; missing, negative, fractional, unsafe, overflow, string, null, unknown-field or otherwise malformed values fail before repository mutation; legacy aliases and defaults are forbidden
requiredSemantics.canonicalCommand: one versioned canonicalizer derives the command digest only from the server-normalized persisted answer facts plus exact session, question, actor and expected revision; submittedAt is excluded so response-loss retries remain stable; order, sparse arrays, duplicate labels and mutable references cannot change the preimage ambiguously
requiredSemantics.cas: new row insert requires expected revision 0 and persists revision 1; update requires current complete revision exactly equal to expected and atomically advances exactly once; current incomplete and legacy rows are never silently upgraded; integer exhaustion fails closed
requiredSemantics.concurrency: two different commands with one expected revision yield one winner and one stable conflict; two identical commands yield one mutation and one exact read-only replay; later updates cannot be overwritten by delayed older requests
requiredSemantics.replay: only current revision equal to expected plus one and exact persisted command digest/facts may be returned as a response-loss replay; any later revision, different answer, actor, session or question is a conflict with zero write
requiredSemantics.authoritativeResponse: repository returns the inserted, updated or replayed persisted row; service and route never return a locally constructed loser or new timestamp; progress and submit responses expose server-owned answerRevision and original persisted submittedAt
requiredSemantics.time: accepted revision timestamps are nondecreasing even if the injected wall clock regresses; stale, replay, invalid authorization, invalid question, malformed answer, corrupt row or CAS conflict does not change submittedAt or updatedAt
requiredSemantics.ui: the learner UI stores each server answerRevision, sends 0 only for a question with no persisted feedback, sends the exact latest revision for an intentional edit, prevents double-submit while in flight and reloads exact progress after a conflict; no client timestamp is authoritative
requiredSemantics.unchanged: authorization, scoring rules, seven question types, subjective review-required behavior, F-0162 immutable session source, public-id boundaries, F-0164 lifecycle/history/statistics and Provider/quota behavior remain unchanged

minimumAdversarialTests: two concurrent first submissions with different answers produce one revision-1 row and one conflict; exactly one mutation occurs
two identical concurrent or response-loss submissions produce one mutation and one read-only replay with the same revision, submittedAt and feedback snapshot
revision 1 to 2 succeeds only with expected 1; delayed expected 0 and competing expected 1 cannot overwrite the committed winner
current partial markers, legacy all-null rows, duplicate or corrupt rows, wrong actor/session/question and revision overflow fail closed with zero write
repository insert/update returning cardinality 0 or greater than 1, digest mismatch and post-conflict reread mismatch do not become success
submittedAt and updatedAt never regress under injected clock rollback; replay preserves the original persisted time
route rejects malformed or missing expectedAnswerRevision before mutation and preserves authorization/not-found masking
UI restores revisions from progress, sends the exact expected revision, blocks double submit and refetches progress after conflict
concurrent session creation still returns only the database winner and F-0162 paper snapshot, seven-type and subjective-answer regressions remain GREEN
migration contains only two nullable columns and one coherence CHECK with no UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, TRUNCATE, backfill or apply

validationCommands.name: state-schema-source-contract
arguments: keeps task state and additive migration source exact

conditions: fresh task-bound database approval is received before RED or product writes
one offline additive migration source is generated and never applied
all validation commands and one main-thread adversarial review are GREEN
final diff stays inside the approved allowed-file envelope
a fresh database token binds the final candidate tree before Kernel/full
base, origin, risk and semantics remain unchanged

previousTaskCloseout.proofBoundary: no migration apply, real database/data or browser/runtime proof

nextReviewHints: request fresh schema/database approval before RED
keep F-0164 pending as an independent lifecycle/history/statistics task

verificationEvidence.jitReadOnlyReview: F0163 creation-loser response is already covered by persisted-winner reread and exact context matching; unconditional answer upsert remains reachable and selected; F0164 remains independent
